title: Lindsay See

infobox.value: * [Patrick Henry College](/wiki/Patrick_Henry_College "Patrick Henry College") ([BA](/wiki/Bachelor_of_Arts "Bachelor of Arts")) * [Harvard University](/wiki/Harvard_Law_School "Harvard Law School") ([JD](/wiki/Juris_Doctor "Juris Doctor"))

is an American lawyer. A [Michigan](/wiki/Michigan "Michigan") native, she attended [Patrick Henry College](/wiki/Patrick_Henry_College "Patrick Henry College"), followed by graduating [magna cum laude](/wiki/Magna_cum_laude "Magna cum laude") at [Harvard Law School](/wiki/Harvard_Law_School "Harvard Law School"), where she was executive editor of the *[Harvard Law Review](/wiki/Harvard_Law_Review "Harvard Law Review")*. Beginning in 2018, she served as [West Virginia](/wiki/West_Virginia "West Virginia")'s [solicitor general](/wiki/Solicitor_general "Solicitor general").[[1]](#cite_note-1) In 2024, President [Joe Biden](/wiki/Joe_Biden "Joe Biden") nominated her for one of the [Republican](/wiki/Republican_Party_(United_States) "Republican Party (United States)") seats on the [Federal Energy Regulatory Commission](/wiki/Federal_Energy_Regulatory_Commission "Federal Energy Regulatory Commission"), at the recommendation of Senate minority leader [Mitch McConnell](/wiki/Mitch_McConnell "Mitch McConnell"). The Senate confirmed her nomination by an 83-12 vote in June 2024.[[2]](#cite_note-2) She previously clerked for Judge [Thomas B. Griffith](/wiki/Thomas_B._Griffith "Thomas B. Griffith") of the [United States Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit](/wiki/United_States_Court_of_Appeals_for_the_District_of_Columbia_Circuit "United States Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit") and spent several years in the [Washington, D.C.](/wiki/Washington,_D.C. "Washington, D.C.") office of [Gibson Dunn](/wiki/Gibson_Dunn "Gibson Dunn").[[3]](#cite_note-3)

["See appointed as W.Va. solicitor general"](https://www.heraldmailmedia.com/story/news/local/2018/06/06/see-appointed-as-wva-solicitor-general/116561998/). *[The Herald-Mail](/wiki/The_Herald-Mail "The Herald-Mail")*. June 6, 2018. Retrieved May 28, 2024.

Tony, Mike (June 13, 2024). ["Senate confirms WV solicitor general to become FERC commissioner"](https://www.wvgazettemail.com/news/energy_and_environment/senate-confirms-wv-solicitor-general-to-become-ferc-commissioner/article_631eaf98-c7b5-5c59-b374-0706d17bb9a5.html). *[Charleston Gazette-Mail](/wiki/Charleston_Gazette-Mail "Charleston Gazette-Mail")*. Retrieved June 24, 2024.

Lewis, Jarett (February 29, 2024). ["State Solicitor General See nominated to Federal Energy Regulatory Commission"](https://wvmetronews.com/2024/02/29/state-solicitor-general-see-nominated-to-federal-energy-regulatory-commission/). *[West Virginia MetroNews](/wiki/West_Virginia_MetroNews "West Virginia MetroNews")*. Retrieved May 28, 2024.

**Lindsay Sara See** is an American lawyer. A [Michigan](/wiki/Michigan "Michigan") native, she attended [Patrick Henry College](/wiki/Patrick_Henry_College "Patrick Henry College"), followed by graduating [magna cum laude](/wiki/Magna_cum_laude "Magna cum laude") at [Harvard Law School](/wiki/Harvard_Law_School "Harvard Law School"), where she was executive editor of the *[Harvard Law Review](/wiki/Harvard_Law_Review "Harvard Law Review")*. Beginning in 2018, she served as [West Virginia](/wiki/West_Virginia "West Virginia")'s [solicitor general](/wiki/Solicitor_general "Solicitor general").[[1]](#cite_note-1) In 2024, President [Joe Biden](/wiki/Joe_Biden "Joe Biden") nominated her for one of the [Republican](/wiki/Republican_Party_(United_States) "Republican Party (United States)") seats on the [Federal Energy Regulatory Commission](/wiki/Federal_Energy_Regulatory_Commission "Federal Energy Regulatory Commission"), at the recommendation of Senate minority leader [Mitch McConnell](/wiki/Mitch_McConnell "Mitch McConnell"). The Senate confirmed her nomination by an 83-12 vote in June 2024.[[2]](#cite_note-2) She previously clerked for Judge [Thomas B. Griffith](/wiki/Thomas_B._Griffith "Thomas B. Griffith") of the [United States Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit](/wiki/United_States_Court_of_Appeals_for_the_District_of_Columbia_Circuit "United States Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit") and spent several years in the [Washington, D.C.](/wiki/Washington,_D.C. "Washington, D.C.") office of [Gibson Dunn](/wiki/Gibson_Dunn "Gibson Dunn").[[3]](#cite_note-3)

Retrieved May 28, 2024.
